runs.summary: Memory index updated — today's Finance activity folded in: 2 market-tracker runs, a live-portfolio review (APH exit, KLR trim, 2 new positions), and a paper-trading P1 update (4 max-hold exits, 1 new trade), plus a new Meta cross-domain connection
content: Domain Content Status — Meta: run count 15→16, added the 2026-07-21 highlight (NY's first-in-nation data-centre moratorium is a new demand-side political risk to idea-bloom-energy and idea-ge-vernova, neither of which addresses it) and noted the 07-20 gap (no thinker run logged that day)

Domain Content Status — Finance: added 07-21 sector-performance-tracker (SPY P/E 26.20x, SMH/XLE/CIBR YTD leaders, CIBR crossed into Expensive P/E band) and growth-value-tracker (spread widened to -1708bps, Q3 partial shows growth selling off again) runs

Domain Content Status — Finance: added the 2026-07-21 trading-portfolio-management review — APH EXIT executed (~€1,492), KLR TRIM 50% executed (~€667), 2 new positions opened (SHEL €1,000, BMY €750); portfolio now 11 positions, cash €813/€2,972 post-settlement

Domain Content Status — Finance: added the 2026-07-21 paper-trading P1 update — AMD added (Strategy E); CAT/IQV/IBM all hit 60-day max hold same day (IQV +17.18%, portfolio's first closed win); LRCX stopped out -18.16%; net P&L fell to +$634 (+1.81%) from +$1,145 (+3.37%) on 07-17

Open Threads: resolved the APH-exit half of the AI-capex-bear-thesis thread (AVGO remains open); marked BMY as actioned and flagged the same-day SHEL entry as previously unflagged; added a new thread on the CAT/IQV/IBM same-day max-hold cluster and the trade log's own entry-pacing concern
Appended one memory-update log line to log.md for 2026-07-21, above today's other entries
